message:
type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: Adding get_image_mimetype() lazy accessor method to ImageBlock class

type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: Extracting normalize_image_bytes helper for image base64 encoding logic in types.py

type: QUESTION
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: I'm adding get_image_mimetype() method + refactoring image_to_base64's mimetype guess to call it; will you also touch the mimetype guessing lines or only the base64 encoding part?

type: ANSWER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: Yes - I added normalize_image_bytes() module-level helper (returns (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)) and refactored image_to_base64 to call it for BOTH the b64encode line and the filetype.guess() line, since the guess must run on decoded bytes not raw self.image. If your get_image_mimetype() also guesses mimetype, please call normalize_image_bytes(self.image)[1] for the bytes to guess on, or coordinate ordering so we don't double-refactor the same 6 lines - let me know your planned diff.

type: INTENT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: My plan: add self.get_image_mimetype() method (checks cache, else filetype.guess(self.image) on raw bytes, caches to self.image_mimetype, returns it). In image_to_base64 I only replace the guess block (current lines 78-80: 'if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(self.image); self.image_mimetype = ...') with a call to self.get_image_mimetype(), called BEFORE the b64encode line. I will not touch the b64encode/normalize_image_bytes line - that's yours. Since guess happens before encoding in the validator, self.image is still raw there, so no decoding needed for that call site.

type: ANSWER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: Heads up: my normalize_image_bytes() detects if self.image is ALREADY base64-encoded (a case the old code didn't handle - it always ran filetype.guess on raw self.image then blindly re-encoded, which double-encodes pre-encoded input and produces wrong mimetype guesses). My image_to_base64 now calls encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image) once, then uses decoded_for_guessing for filetype.guess and encoded_image for self.image assignment - both lines come from one call, so please don't independently re-add filetype.guess(self.image) on raw bytes in get_image_mimetype(), or you'll reintroduce the pre-encoded-input bug. Suggest your get_image_mimetype() internally call normalize_image_bytes(self.image)[1] to get correct bytes to guess on. Let me know if that works for you - I'm finalizing my patch now.

type: STATUS
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: Note: repo working tree is still clean (git diff empty) on my end - I don't see your normalize_image_bytes edits landed yet. To keep my diff minimal and independently correct, I'm implementing get_image_mimetype() to mirror the ORIGINAL guess semantics (guess on self.image, called before encoding, from within image_to_base64) rather than depending on a helper that doesn't exist in the file yet. I'll only replace the 3-line inline guess block (old lines 78-80) with a call to self.get_image_mimetype() and add the new method after image_to_base64 - I won't touch the b64encode line or add any pre-encoded-input detection. If you need the guess to run on decoded bytes for your own bug fix, please have your normalize_image_bytes call self.get_image_mimetype() or keep its own guess logic separate from mine to avoid double-editing the same lines.

type: STATUS
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: Done: added get_image_mimetype() method and refactored image_to_base64's inline guess into a call to it (lines ~75-94). Tests pass (12/12 in tests/base/llms/test_types.py). Wrote patch.txt. I did not touch the b64encode line's structure beyond keeping it right after the get_image_mimetype() call - final merge with your normalize_image_bytes change is up to you/the harness.
